mnemonics: SEE: Source is Encoded, target is Decoded
A Bilingual Babelfish: Encoder listens in one language, Decoder speaks in another.
Remember ATT: Attention is the Translator's Tool. It helps the decoder focus on the right parts of the source sentence.
keyPoints: The Encoder processes the input sequence and compresses it into a context vector (a fixed-length representation).
The Decoder takes the context vector and generates the output sequence token by token, often using an autoregressive process.
Attention mechanisms allow the decoder to focus on different parts of the input sequence for each step of the output generation, vastly improving performance on long sequences.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to teacher-force the decoder during training, leading to unstable learning.
Assuming the context vector alone can perfectly capture all information from a very long input sequence, which is why attention is critical.
Confusing the training phase (using the true target as input to the decoder) with the inference phase (using the decoder's own previous output as input).
